spec_match.rationale: The side-by-side stream exercises the motivating high-volume conversion use case and posts both capped and uncapped registrations to the backend route. DevTools MCP verified 35 conversions without ctx_id accepted 20 and dropped 15, while the same flow with ctx_id accepted 35 and dropped 0.

interactive.rationale: The conversion slider, trigger_context_id text field, and re-run button drive backend requests, per-conversion row states, the validation badge, and the summary.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page promises a capped batch versus per-conversion stream comparison and now renders both the live accepted/dropped streams and the actual response headers for each column.

edge_cases.rationale: Backend rejection and malformed responses are caught and surfaced as a fallback, while invalid header-unsafe ctx_id values are blocked locally. DevTools MCP verified an emoji ctx_id shows invalid state and is not sent to the backend.

references.rationale: Includes the ChromeStatus feature link and the trigger_context_id spec anchor.

design_system.rationale: Uses design-system variables, explicit contrast for status pills, and no document-level horizontal overflow under 390px mobile emulation.

summary: Re-reviewed in Chrome 148 through chrome-devtools-mcp. Exercised default 35-conversion flow, invalid emoji ctx_id, and 12-conversion valid ctx_id; backend headers and summaries updated correctly. Console/network were clean apart from expected localhost Attribution Reporting attestation warnings.
